NAME

system.c - 🔧 Shared cross-platform system utilities (safe string functions, etc.)

Detailed Description

🔧 Shared cross-platform system utilities (safe string functions, etc.)

Definition in file system.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define BIN_SUFFIX ''

Definition at line 27 of file system.c.

#define PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH 4096

Maximum path length supported by the operating system

Platform-specific values:

  • Windows: 32767 characters (extended-length path with \?\ prefix)
  • Linux: 4096 bytes (PATH_MAX from limits.h)
  • macOS: 1024 bytes (PATH_MAX from sys/syslimits.h)

Note: Windows legacy MAX_PATH (260) is too restrictive for modern use. We use the extended-length limit instead.

Definition at line 66 of file system.c.

void platform_cleanup_binary_path_cache (void )

Cleanup the binary PATH cache.

Definition at line 226 of file system.c.

226                                               {
227   if (!lifecycle_shutdown(&g_cache_lc)) {
228     return;
229   }
230 
231   if (g_bin_path_cache) {
232     rwlock_wrlock(&g_cache_rwlock);
233 
234     // Free all cached entries using uthash iteration
235     bin_cache_entry_t *entry, *tmp;
236     HASH_ITER(hh, g_bin_path_cache, entry, tmp) {
237       HASH_DELETE(hh, g_bin_path_cache, entry);
238       free_cache_entry(entry);
239     }
240 
241     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_cache_rwlock);
242     rwlock_destroy(&g_cache_rwlock);
243     g_bin_path_cache = NULL;
244   }
245 }




References lifecycle_shutdown().






int
  safe_fprintf (FILE * stream, const char * format, ...)


Safe formatted output to file stream.


Parameters


stream Output file stream


format Printf-style format string


Returns


Number of characters written, or -1 on error


Safely formats and prints output to a file stream. Returns the
    number of characters written, or -1 on error.


Definition at line 287 of file system.c.


287                                                         {
288   if (!stream || !format) {
289     return -1;
290   }
291 
292   va_list args;
293   va_start(args, format);
294   int ret = vfprintf(stream, format, args);
295   va_end(args);
296 
297   return ret;
298 }

int
  safe_snprintf (char * buffer, size_t buffer_size, const char * format,
  ...)


Safe formatted string printing to buffer.


Parameters


buffer Output buffer


buffer_size Size of output buffer


format Printf-style format string


Returns


Number of characters written, or -1 on error


Safely formats a string into a buffer with bounds checking. Uses
    platform_snprintf for cross-platform implementation. Returns the number of
    characters written (not including null terminator). Returns -1 if buffer is
    too small.


Definition at line 263 of file system.c.


263                                                                              {
264   if (!buffer || !format || buffer_size == 0) {
265     return -1;
266   }
267 
268   va_list args;
269   va_start(args, format);
270 
271   /* Delegate to platform_snprintf for actual formatting */
272   int ret = platform_vsnprintf(buffer, buffer_size, format, args);
273 
274   va_end(args);
275   return ret;
276 }






References args, buffer_size, and
    platform_vsnprintf().

int
  safe_vsnprintf (char * buffer, size_t buffer_size, const char * format,
  va_list ap)


Safe formatted string printing with va_list.


Parameters


buffer Output buffer (can be NULL if buffer_size
  is 0 for size calculation)


buffer_size Size of output buffer


format Printf-style format string


ap Variable argument list


Returns


Number of characters written, or -1 on error


Safely formats a string into a buffer with bounds checking using
    va_list. Uses platform_vsnprintf for cross-platform implementation. Returns
    the number of characters written (not including null terminator). Special
    case: NULL buffer with size 0 returns required buffer size for size
    calculation. Returns -1 if buffer is too small, format is invalid, or buffer
    is NULL with size > 0.


Definition at line 314 of file system.c.


314                                                                                      {
315   if (!format) {
316     return -1;
317   }
318 
319   // Allow NULL buffer with size 0 for size calculation (standard vsnprintf behavior)
320   if (!buffer && buffer_size > 0) {
321     return -1; // Non-NULL buffer required when buffer_size > 0
322   }
323 
324   /* Delegate to platform_vsnprintf for actual formatting */
325   return platform_vsnprintf(buffer, buffer_size, format, ap);
326 }






References buffer_size, and
  platform_vsnprintf().


Referenced by disconnect_client_for_bad_data(),
    format_message(), log_file_msg(), log_mmap_write(),
    log_msg(), and log_plain_msg().
